title: Immunization counseling
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Requires screening before each dose and identifies severe previous reaction, acute illness severity, and immunosuppression-related precautions.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to clinician-assessed vaccine-specific contraindication and precaution documentation without making an eligibility decision.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires screening before each dose and identifies severe previous reaction, acute illness severity, and immunosuppression-related precautions.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

investigations.text: Defines consent context, scheduled-dose and history checks, illness, previous severe reaction, immune status, medication and recent-vaccine screening.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to recording consent, screening and interview context without authorizing administration.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines consent context, scheduled-dose and history checks, illness, previous severe reaction, immune status, medication and recent-vaccine screening.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 15. Recommendation Eleven: Vaccine Administration and Recording — consent, preparation, screening, and interview questions

assessment.text: Defines DHA immunization scope and the national childhood programme and schedule context.
assessment.rationale: Defines the DHA immunization context and child-program boundary; it cannot support a universal schedule.
assessment.evidence_extract: Defines DHA immunization scope and the national childhood programme and schedule context.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5. Childhood Immunization Program 0 to 6 Years

medication.text: Requires review of the child vaccination history, use of age-appropriate catch-up scheduling, and completion without restarting a series solely because of elapsed time.
medication.rationale: Relevant to documenting prior-dose and catch-up history only when the child and schedule context are established.
medication.evidence_extract: Requires review of the child vaccination history, use of age-appropriate catch-up scheduling, and completion without restarting a series solely because of elapsed time.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

exact_section.heading: 5.8 Catch-Up Immunization for Children Aged 4 Months Through 6 Years

safety_netting.text: Requires benefits and risks discussion and after-care information on common effects, when to seek attention, and when to notify the facility.
safety_netting.rationale: Relevant to documenting education actually given; it does not generate counselling content automatically.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Requires benefits and risks discussion and after-care information on common effects, when to seek attention, and when to notify the facility.

source.title: Guidelines for Best Practice in Immunization

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Vaccine, product, dose history, age, eligibility, pregnancy, immune status, contraindications, consent and current schedule are unspecified.
No vaccine selection, scheduling, dosing, contraindication conclusion or administration is generated.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
